spec_match.rationale: Builds an iOS-style icon grid with `corner-shape: squircle` and chat bubbles using per-corner properties (`corner-bottom-*-shape`). Both are headline motivations the spec calls out — iOS icons cannot be faked with border-radius alone.

interactive.rationale: Working toggle button (1 listener) flips the grid between round and squircle, with a feature-detect probe at the top. CSS-driven interactivity is allowed for CSS features per CLAUDE.md.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `an iOS-style app icon grid you can flip from round to squircle, and chat bubbles with one scooped tail corner via the per-corner corner-bottom-*-shape properties` — page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Two motivations covered well, but the page doesn't degrade visibly on browsers without `corner-shape` support — the probe sets a banner but the grid still shows fallback border-radius which looks similar to the squircle to a casual eye.

references.rationale: `see also` cites both the chromestatus link and the css-borders-4 corner-shaping anchor.

design_system.rationale: 63 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Make the unsupported-browser fallback unambiguous
openQuestions.detail: When `corner-shape` is unsupported, the icons fall back to `border-radius` which still looks rounded — readers may not realise the demo isn't working. Add a side-by-side `with vs without` reference so the difference is unmistakable even in the fallback case.

openQuestions.title: Add a third per-corner example
openQuestions.detail: Chat bubbles use the bottom-* per-corner properties. Add an example using `corner-top-left-shape`/`corner-top-right-shape` to demonstrate the full longhand surface — currently only two of the eight longhand combinations are visible.

openQuestions.title: Show real iOS-icon dimensions
openQuestions.detail: iOS uses a specific superellipse curve (close to `superellipse(5)`). Annotate the squircle grid with the actual exponent Apple uses so authors can replicate iOS icons exactly instead of just `something roundish`.

summary: Two canonical motivations rendered together — best concept page for telling the `why this exists` story. Add a clearer unsupported-fallback contrast and an iOS-exact exponent annotation and it becomes the reference demo of the feature.
